to roast, to parch (am Feuer röften), as corn, grain, Lev 2:14 Jos 5:11 a person, as a mode of execution, Jer 29:22. (Arab. قلى, Eth. ቀለወ፡ id., compare צָלָה and last remark under the letter צ p. 698, A .) Part. pass. קָלוּי Lev 2:14 Jos 5:11.

Niphal, part. what is scorched; hence, burning, inflammation, Psa 38:8.

Derivatives, קָלִי, and pr.n. מִקְלֹות. [This pr.n. should be referred to מָקַל, as it is in its own place, and in Thes.]

II.

קלה

i.q. קָלַל, not used in Kal.

Niphal, to be made light of, Isa 16:14 to be counted despicable, Deu 25:3 part. נִקְלֶה despised, ignoble, 1Sa 18:23 Isa 3:5 Pro 12:9.

Hiphil, to make light of, Deu 27:16. Hence-
